content.content: Check all of my repositories and star any that have zero stars.

<function_thought>
To fulfill the user's request for checking all repositories and starring those with zero stars, I will begin by fetching the logged-in GitHub user's information to obtain their repositories. This will enable me to identify which repositories have zero stars. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_logged_in_github_user_information()]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
I have successfully retrieved the user's information. The user has the following repositories:
1. emmajohnson/file_compression (1 star)
2. emmajohnson/image_transformation (0 stars)
3. emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation (0 stars)

Now, I will proceed to check the starred repositories and then identify and star any repositories that have zero stars, specifically "emmajohnson/image_transformation" and "emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation".
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_star(repo_name="emmajohnson/image_transformation"), git_star(repo_name="emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
The repositories "emmajohnson/image_transformation" and "emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation" have been successfully starred. This completes the user's request to check all repositories and star any that had zero stars.
</function_thought>

<final_answer>
The following repositories have been successfully starred:
1. emmajohnson/image_transformation
2. emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation
</final_answer>
